notes: Hand-curated PK + vitals-effects rows. Each medication row carries pk_source + pk_evidence_url. RxCUIs populated where canonical id is unambiguous; remaining null until import-rxnorm script runs (Session 2).

rows.description: Sympathomimetic - increases HR and BP
Anticholinergic - increases HR; antidote for organophosphate / muscarinic toxicity
Antiarrhythmic - causes transient AV block
Class III antiarrhythmic - slows HR, mild hypotension
Class IB antiarrhythmic - VT/VF; CNS toxicity at high doses
Class IA antiarrhythmic - prolongs QRS/QT; stop if hypotension
Calcium channel blocker - rate control in AF/SVT
Calcium channel blocker - SVT termination; avoid in WPW
Cardiac glycoside - rate control + inotropy; narrow therapeutic window
Beta-1 blocker - decreases HR and BP
Ultra-short-acting beta-1 blocker
Alpha agonist - first-line vasopressor in septic shock
Dose-dependent: dopaminergic < 5, beta 5-10, alpha > 10 mcg/kg/min
Non-catecholamine vasopressor (V1) - septic shock adjunct
Pure alpha-1 agonist - reflex bradycardia common
Beta-1 inotrope - cardiogenic shock; can drop SVR/BP at high dose
PDE-3 inhibitor - inodilator; renal dose adjustment
Alpha/beta blocker - hypertensive emergency, pre-eclampsia
Direct vasodilator - reflex tachycardia; pregnancy-safe
Venodilator - reduces preload; tolerance with continuous use
Ultra-short-acting dihydropyridine CCB - lipid emulsion
Dihydropyridine CCB - smooth titration in hypertensive emergency
ACE inhibitor IV - avoid in pregnancy and bilateral renal artery stenosis
Opioid - histamine release, respiratory depression
Synthetic opioid - chest-wall rigidity at high dose
Semi-synthetic opioid - 5-7x potency of morphine
Antipyretic + analgesic; max 4 g/day
NSAID - max 5 days; renal/GI risk
Benzodiazepine - sedation, respiratory depression
Benzodiazepine - first-line for status epilepticus
GABA-A agonist - significant hypotension; PRIS at high dose long duration
RSI induction - hemodynamically neutral; transient adrenal suppression
NMDA antagonist - dissociative; bronchodilator; preserves BP
Non-depolarizing NMBA - RSI; reversible with sugammadex
Depolarizing NMBA - hyperK risk; avoid in burns, crush, denervation
Anticoagulant - prolongs aPTT; reversal with protamine
LMWH - factor Xa inhibition; renal dose adjustment
Vitamin K antagonist - INR-titrated; reversal with vitamin K / 4F-PCC
Thrombolytic - acute ischemic stroke <4.5h, STEMI, massive PE
Single-bolus thrombolytic - fibrin-specific
COX-1 antiplatelet - chew for ACS
P2Y12 antiplatelet - load 300-600 mg for PCI
Reversible P2Y12 antiplatelet - dyspnea side effect
Opioid antagonist - reverses respiratory depression; short half-life vs many opioids
Benzodiazepine antagonist - seizure risk in chronic BZD users
Heparin reversal - hypotension if rapid; anaphylaxis risk in fish-allergy/diabetics
Warfarin reversal - slow IV; anaphylactoid risk if rapid
Factor Xa inhibitor reversal (apixaban/rivaroxaban) - thrombotic risk
Dabigatran reversal monoclonal Fab
Acetaminophen antidote - 21h IV protocol; anaphylactoid in first hour
Cholinesterase reactivator - organophosphate poisoning; pair with atropine
Lead poisoning chelator - nephrotoxic; ensure hydration
Digoxin toxicity reversal - rapid restoration of conduction
Somatostatin analogue - sulfonylurea OD, variceal bleed
DKA/HHS infusion; hypoglycemia + hypokalemia monitoring
Long-acting basal insulin - peakless profile
Intermediate-acting insulin - peaks 4-12h
Rapid-acting prandial insulin
Hypoglycemia rescue - extravasation causes tissue necrosis
Hypoglycemia + beta-blocker overdose; nausea common
Adrenal insufficiency / refractory shock
Severe asthma/COPD exacerbation, anaphylaxis (delayed)
Torsades, severe asthma, eclampsia; respiratory depression at high levels
Membrane stabilization in hyperkalemia; CCB/BB overdose
3x Ca content of gluconate; central line preferred (extravasation necrosis)
Severe acidosis, TCA / Na-channel blocker toxicity, hyperkalemia
Replace hypokalemia; max peripheral 10 mEq/hr; ECG monitor
Severe hypophosphatemia; risk of hyperK + hypoCa
3rd-gen cephalosporin - meningitis, CAP, pyelonephritis
MRSA coverage; trough or AUC monitoring; red-man syndrome if rapid
Broad-spectrum beta-lactam - HAP, intra-abdominal sepsis
4th-gen cephalosporin - febrile neutropenia, Pseudomonas
Carbapenem - ESBL, severe sepsis empiric
Respiratory fluoroquinolone - QT prolongation, tendinopathy
Macrolide - atypical coverage in CAP
Anaerobic + protozoal - C. diff, intra-abdominal
5-HT3 antagonist - QT prolongation
D2 antagonist + prokinetic - EPS / dystonia risk
Phenothiazine antiemetic - boxed warning for tissue necrosis if extravasation
Beta-2 agonist - bronchodilation, mild tachycardia, hypokalemia
Anticholinergic bronchodilator - synergistic with albuterol
Status epilepticus second-line; well-tolerated, renal dose adjustment
Status epilepticus - max 50 mg/min; hypotension, purple-glove
Loop diuretic - reduces preload; ototoxic if rapid
Loop diuretic - 40x potency of furosemide
K-sparing aldosterone antagonist - HFrEF, ascites; hyperkalemia
Crystalloid - volume expansion
Crystalloid - significant volume expansion
Balanced crystalloid - sepsis-preferred
Free water replacement - no oncotic / volume support
Colloid - oncotic pressure support; SAFE trial
Improves venous return; may compromise breathing
Improves breathing, reduces preload
Maximum breathing comfort, reduces preload
Neutral position
Improves cardiac output in pregnancy
